title: Kopa Khan
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Deity |image= |name=Kopa Khan |religion=[[Lortan Fanatics]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-10-the-rise-of-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 10: The Rise of the Empire}} |pantheon= |type= |powers= |worship= |artifacts= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' |pronouns= |form=[[Human/Legends|Man]]-[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]] |first= |area=[[Lorta/Legends|Lorta]] |affiliation= }} {{Quote|On the pyramids is a legend, the great god Kopa Khan who came from the stars and vanished, and the legends say he will come back again someday.|[[Unidentified Human singer (Blockade of Kashyyyk)|Unidentified Human singer]]|}} '''Kopa Khan''' was a [[God/Legends|deity]] worshipped by the [[Lortan Fanatics]] who instigated the [[Reslian Purge]]. He was described as a [[Human/Legends|Man]]-[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] hybrid, and expected by his cult to remove evil from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. After the end of the Reslian Purge, the song "[[Light the Sky on Fire]]," which referenced the legend of Kopa Khan, was popular on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. ==History== The [[Lortan Fanatics]] who ravaged the [[Yushan sector/Legends|Yushan sector]] in the [[Reslian Purge]] were described by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] propaganda as "Kopa Khan cultists." The god they worshipped was a [[Human/Legends|Man]]-[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] who they believed would banish evil from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. "[[Light the Sky on Fire]]," a song sung by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Unidentified Human band|band]] popular on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] circa [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-part-2|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} also made reference to the "Great [[God/Legends|God]] Kopa Khan". The song mentioned that Kopa Khan arrived from the [[Star/Legends|stars]], and then vanished. The song also mentioned that his return was prophesied. ==Behind the scenes== This deity's first mention in ''[[Star Wars]]'' material comes during [[Jefferson Starship]]'s performance of "Light the Sky on Fire" in ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''. The deity is mentioned during a spoken-word break between verses. However, in the absence of an official script or subtitled home video release of the ''Holiday Special'', the name of this deity as spoken by lead singer [[Marty Balin]] is difficult to determine. The liner notes to the CD release of Jefferson Starship's [[Wikipedia:Gold (Jefferson Starship album)|''Gold'']], an album which includes "Light the Sky on Fire", do not include lyrics which would establish the spelling of the deity's name. The name "Kopa Khan" is found in a [http://www.starwarsholidayspecial.com/text/swhs-transcript.txt transcript] of the ''Holiday Special'' made in [[1995]] by a fan named John Haller. In an introductory note discussing "Light the Sky on Fire", Haller says "A couple of the lines of this song are just guessed at."John Haller, [http://www.starwarsholidayspecial.com/text/swhs-transcript.txt THE STAR WARS HOLIDAY SPECIAL TRANSCRIPT Version 1.0], hosted on [http://www.starwarsholidayspecial.com/default.htm StarWarsHolidaySpecial.com] According to the lyrics of the song, as transcribed by Haller:
''On the pyramids of the legend. /The great god Kopa Khan came from the stars and vanished. / And the legends say he will come back again someday.''
Kopa Khan was mentioned for the first time in written ''Star Wars'' material in part 10 of ''[[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut]]''. [[Paul R. Urquhart]], co-author of ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', indicated on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] that Kopa Khan from the ''Holiday Special'' should be identified with the Man-[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] worshipped by the Lortan Fanatics which [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]] refers to.[[Paul R. Urquhart]] (writing as "Thrawn McEwok"), [[Jedi Council Forums]], thread "The Official StarWars.com Blog Thread - That's right, I went there", [http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-starwars-com-blog-thread-thats-right-i-went-there.50015309/page-46#post-51719258 post #1135]. Elements of the description of "Kopa Khan" in "Light the Sky on Fire", such as the association with pyramids, the celestial origins, and the prophecy of return, are consistent with [[Wikipedia:Kukulkan|Kukulkan]], the feathered serpent god from Mayan mythology.The Mayans are the same civilization which built the pyramid at [[Tikal]] used during the filming of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] to represent [[Great Temple/Legends|the Rebel base on Yavin IV]]. In response to a fan asking about a connection between Kopa Khan and Kukulkan, Urquhart responded that he did not specifically mean to make the connection. Urquhart did note a parallel between the Man-Hutt in ''Rogue Squadron 25'' and God-Emperor Leto Atreides II, a main character in Frank Herbert's ''Dune'' series.[[Paul R. Urquhart]] (writing as "Thrawn McEwok"), [[Jedi Council Forums]], thread "The Official StarWars.com Blog Thread - That's right, I went there", [http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-starwars-com-blog-thread-thats-right-i-went-there.50015309/page-46#post-51721287 post #1139].In [[Wikipedia:Children of Dune|''Children of Dune'']], Herbert's third ''Dune'' novel, Leto II becomes a hybrid between human and sandworm. By the time of the fourth novel, Leto II has led a religious crusade which has brought known space under his control, and reigned for thousands of years as the titular [[Wikipedia:God Emperor of Dune|''God Emperor of Dune'']]. ==Appearances== *''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' {{1stm}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 25]] {{Imo}} {{Flash}} ==Sources== *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-10-the-rise-of-the-empire|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 10: The Rise of the Empire}} {{1stID|Kopa Khan}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Deities]]
